page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an x86_64 architecture. It features 288 dedicated vCPUs derived from 144 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 2232.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 7.75 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 504 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute-intensive workloads. The instance achieves top 10% placement in multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in cryptographic tasks, software compilation, and static web serving throughput. However, certain performance tradeoffs exist. While prompt processing speeds for large language models (LLMs) fall into the top 10% or top 25% brackets, text generation speeds for smaller models are weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Additionally, the PassMark memory score registers in the average middle 50% tier, contrasting with the high-throughput read and write bandwidth results.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-288 offers high resource density, combining massive memory capacity with a high vCPU count on a single node. This configuration reduces the need for distributed clustering in memory-heavy applications, though the lack of local storage requires external block storage provisioning. The instance is suited for large-scale database operations, memory-intensive data processing, software compilation pipelines, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 288 dedicated vCPUs and 2232.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor at 2.3 GHz with a 504 MB L3 cache and support for Advanced Matrix Extensions. The instance features 144 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, delivering 7.75 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth. Tradeoffs include average PassMark memory scores and weak text generation speeds for smaller LLM models, despite top-tier prompt processing performance. Qualitatively, the server provides high resource density for memory-heavy workloads, eliminating clustering overhead. It is suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.

tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 288 dedicated vCPUs for large-scale database and compute-intensive enterprise workloads.

Top-tier multi-core CPU, database operations, and memory bandwidth benchmarks.
